text:
Market today: Risk-off tone is building. Iran drone attacks on shipping plus new tariff threats on digital services taxes are squeezing exactly the growth/tech names you're concentrated in. When macro fear spikes, chips and high-multiple tech get hit hardest - and margin amplifies every down-tick.

Your holdings: AVGO is the alarm - your largest position, down 15.8%, and ON Semi just cratered 24% on a chip deal gone wrong, which poisons sentiment across semis including AVGO and SMH. PLTR is down 13.3% with no obvious floor catalyst right now. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - tiny move against you and your stop at $150 is right there; treat it like nitroglycerin on margin.

Watch: 1) AVGO holding above its $336 stop - if semis keep bleeding post-ON Semi shock, that level gets tested fast. 2) Whether tariff/Iran headlines escalate into tomorrow - two macro fires burning at once is unusual.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is AVGO dragging your whole book lower while you're on margin - know exactly what you'll do if $336 breaks.
